change_comments.message:
even I checked by running 2 instances.
the results were coming out from one instance I guess. and when I paused/stopped the first container the results start to come out from the second container.
Anyways we have commented config. for extra ordinary times we can as well uncomment the code and look metrics for both containers.

inline_comments.message:
as we were discussing on the chat.
wanted to check which server metrics will be shown by the dashboard when we look at it ??
first one , second one , mixed ?? hope it wont lead us to incorrect observations.

In a Docker Compose setup with multiple replicas, Docker’s internal DNS resolves the service name (e.g., cps-and-ncmp) to one of the running replicas. Then, Prometheus will only scrape the service that's accessible via the default service name, which is typically tied to just one of the replicas at any given time.

Thus, there will be no incorrect observation but selecting either of replicas.

Otherwise, individuals need to reset the targets according to their docker-compose version and dynamic container naming.
